notes: Antigravity long-idle survival, UNBLOCKED by PID binding via history.jsonl. The conv-id session resolves its real workspace cwd from ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/history.jsonl (antigravity_cwd.go) so DiscoverPID binds pid>0; the readyTTL sweep then SKIPS the session while its process is alive (pid_manager.go:790-794), so it survives idle past readyTTL and resumes on the next prompt. Single-birth transcript-first adapter: SessionIDFromPath derives the session id from the <conv-id> directory, so the session is born directly in ready when transcript.jsonl appears (no transient proc-<PID> presession row). Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so the 15000ms idle floor cleanly exceeds the TTL. If the PID-liveness hold regresses, the idle_window invariants fire (no transcript_removed, no flicker to working). Model is harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE>; this account reports 'Gemini 3.1 Pro'. Tokens/cost are NOT in the antigravity JSONL.

text: Conv-id-keyed session appears in ready after the first turn writes transcript.jsonl; the real workspace cwd (history.jsonl) lets DiscoverPID bind the agy PID (pid>0)
First user prompt (USER_INPUT) drives ready → working
Terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls settles the session back to ready
Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL — the pid>0 liveness check holds it in ready; state remains ready during the idle
Next user prompt: ready → working resumes normally on the SAME session
Second turn completes back to ready
